Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Based on the event log cases 2003 and 2005 stand out as taking significantly longer over two and three days respectively due to multiple requests for additional documents

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: These are both high complexity claims with 2003 handled in Region A and 2005 in Region B

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The root cause seems tied to complexity high complexity claims trigger repeated document requests extending the process

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Interestingly the high complexity case in Region A 2003 took about two days while the one in Region B 2005 took over three days possibly due to more requests or delays in approvals by Manager Bill

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Resources like Adjuster Lisa in 2005 requested documents three times compared to Adjuster Mike s two in 2003 suggesting possible skill or efficiency differences

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Manager Bill s approvals also appear slower with waits of nearly a day after requests unlike Manager Ann s quicker 20 hour turnaround for medium cases

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: To mitigate implement a more thorough initial evaluation for high complexity claims to reduce document requests

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Provide training for Adjusters on handling complex cases efficiently and consider workload management for Managers to ensure timely approvals
